Based off the plan, what would you say are some good atomic next steps

Here are **atomic next steps** you can take one at a time, aligned with the plan and milestones:
---
## 1. **Naming / branding (low risk)**
- **Decide and apply title** — Choose whether to keep "Neon DrifterInfinite" or rename (e.g. drop "Infinite", or a newdark-fantasy name). Do a single find/replace in `index.html` (and any docs) so the visible title and comments match the decision. Nogameplay change.---
## 2. **PWA basics (M5, but independent)**- **Add `manifest.json`** — App name, short_name,icons (or placeholders), `start_url`, `display: standalone`. One new file, no code refactor.
- **Add minimal serviceworker** — Register a SW that caches `index.html` (andany critical assets) on install. Keep it minimalso the app isinstallable and cacheable. Can be done without touching game logic.
These two can be separate commits.
---
## 3. **Bounded world (M2) — split into small steps**
- **Lock camera to world** —Replace infinitehorizontal scroll with a fixed world width (e.g. constant orfrom a simpleconfig). Camera stays within that width; no new rooms yet.- **Define “one zone”** — Pick a format(single room, or a few hand-placed platforms in a fixed layout). Implement one explorablezone (e.g. onescreen or one chunk) with that format.
- **Add world bounds in physics** — Set Phaser world bounds to that zone(or full map) sothe player and camera never leave the playable area.
You can do “camera + one zone + bounds” in one or two small steps.
---## 4. **Firstability gate (M3) — two atoms**
- **Implement one ability** — e.g. double jump or dash: input handling, state (e.g. “can double jump”), and the movement effect. No gates yet.
-**Add one gate** — One place in the first zone that isunreachable without that ability (e.g. highledge or gap). When the playerhas the ability, they canreach it. No save/load required for this step.---
## 5.**Tests**
- **After any RNG or pit/terrain change**— Add or update tests in `./tests/` and run `node tests/game-logic.test.js` (and CI). Treatthis as a recurring atomic stepwhenever you touch those systems.
---
## Suggested order
| Order | Step |Why |
|-------|------|-----|
| 1 | Naming decision + apply | Quick, avoids confusion as you add features. |
| 2 | `manifest.json` | One file, norefactor, moves you toward PWA. |
| 3 | Minimal service worker | Makes the app installable; independent of game design. |
|4 | Camera + world bounds + one zone | Core of M2; enables “explorable map” instead of infinite runner. || 5 | First ability | Unlocks M3. |
| 6 | First gate | Completes “ability-gated progression” for one place. |
